title: Counterpoint: 2026Q1 NAND revenue hit a record on enterprise SSD demand
summary: Counterpoint data cited by PC Gamer says NAND industry revenue in 2026Q1 reached a record level, with enterprise SSDs becoming the core demand driver for AI data centers. This supports the view that the current cycle is led by data-center storage rather than PC or handset replacement.

title: TrendForce: 2026Q2 NAND contract prices expected to rise faster than DRAM
summary: TrendForce data cited by Tom's Hardware points to another sharp NAND contract-price increase in 2026Q2, driven by enterprise SSD allocation, cloud-provider long-term commitments, and limited near-term capacity additions.

title: Silicon Motion interview: NAND supply is being redirected from retail SSDs to AI data centers
summary: Silicon Motion management described a structural shift in SSD demand: memory suppliers are prioritizing AI data-center and OEM allocation, while retail SSD supply has shrunk. This highlights SSD controllers and module makers as second-order beneficiaries.

title: Micron 9650 and 6600 ION: G9 NAND, PCIe Gen6, and high-capacity QLC SSD roadmap
summary: Micron's data-center SSD portfolio cited by Tom's Hardware shows how leading NAND vendors are moving toward high-speed PCIe Gen6 SSDs and high-density QLC SSDs for AI and hyperscale workloads.

title: Micron exits Crucial consumer business to prioritize enterprise and AI customers
summary: Micron's wind-down of the Crucial consumer SSD and memory brand, reported by Tom's Hardware, is evidence that large suppliers are reallocating output away from low-margin consumer channels toward strategic enterprise and AI demand.

title: SanDisk enterprise NAND pricing and AI storage demand
summary: Nomura checks cited by Tom's Hardware said enterprise-grade NAND pricing pressure intensified in 2026Q1, with AI storage architectures and hyperscaler procurement as key drivers. This supports SanDisk as a high-beta pure NAND candidate, but the evidence is broker-channel based.

title: SanDisk became independent again after Western Digital flash spin-off
summary: Public company background cited in market coverage and reference data shows SanDisk was separated from Western Digital as an independent flash-storage company, making SNDK a more direct listed NAND exposure than WDC.

title: SK hynix 2026Q1 results: strong demand and supply discipline
summary: MarketWatch coverage of SK hynix 2026Q1 results says revenue and earnings rose sharply, and management expected demand to outstrip supply for multiple years. This supports the memory-upcycle thesis but must be separated from HBM-specific strength.

title: SK hynix 321-layer 2Tb QLC NAND mass production
summary: SK hynix's 321-layer QLC NAND roadmap cited by Tom's Hardware shows the technical direction of high-capacity SSDs, including potential 244TB-class enterprise products. This is relevant to Solidigm and AI data-center storage density.

title: Samsung and SK hynix warn of multi-year AI-driven memory shortages
summary: Industry coverage of Samsung and SK hynix earnings commentary points to multi-year tightness across memory categories, with enterprise SSD demand rising alongside HBM. The source is secondary, so use it as triangulation rather than sole proof.

title: Longsys and Lexar: AI-ready storage, controller work, and module manufacturing
summary: Tom's Hardware's 2026 visit to Lexar and Longsys describes product and manufacturing shifts toward AI-ready storage, controller development, and high-end module differentiation. This supports Longsys as an A-share NAND module candidate but not as a NAND wafer producer.

title: STAR paper: higher-capacity 3D NAND creates new reliability constraints
summary: A 2025 storage-architecture paper based on real 3D NAND characterization argues that high-capacity modern SSDs face retention and reliability issues that require stronger controller and firmware techniques. This supports the scarcity of high-end SSD controller and firmware capability.
